title: CM Adityanath tells recruitment boards to avoid making undignified remarks on any community
summary: The controversy comes days after Opposition parties accused the BJP government in Uttar Pradesh of being "anti-Brahmin" following the alleged mistreatment of Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati during the Magh Mela in Prayagraj.

title: Odisha BJP shifts its MLAs to Paradip ahead of Rajya Sabha polls
summary: The BJP MLAs, including Ministers, boarded two luxury buses to the port town after a crucial meeting with senior party leaders at the BJP state party headquarters on Saturday (March 14, 2026).

title: In frames | War lights a kitchen fire
summary: West Asian conflict has snuffed out a clean cooking fuel in kitchens; facing a crisis, homes and eateries are looking at firewood and biomass to energise their cooking

title: Watch: HPV vaccination roll out, climate change, metabolic diseases and more | Health Wrap
summary: In this episode of Health Wrap, senior health journalists Ramya Kannan and Zubeda Hamid discuss the week’s important developments in public health. This episode looks at the rollout of Human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccination across States in India to prevent cervical cancer, which continues to claim thousands of lives each year.

title: ECI to announce Assembly poll dates for 4 States, one U.T. today
summary: The poll authority has convened a press conference at 4 p.m. to announce the schedule. The terms of these Legislative Assemblies are ending on different dates in May and June

title: Fire breaks out at Nature Bazaar in south Delhi, around 50 shops gutted
summary: According to the DFS, a call regarding the blaze was received at 7.37 a.m., following which 10 fire tenders were rushed to the spot to control the flames. No injury to anyone has been reported so far.

title: India well-placed to deal with any situation on crude oil, fuel amid West Asia supply shock: Piyush Goyal
summary: Acknowledging that while the country is facing "stress" on the rupee, Mr. Goyal said there is no reason for anyone to panic as rupee is expected to bounce back in a more gradual fashion, possibly once the war is over

title: Fact check: Are “40 out of 100 children malnourished” in Gujarat?
summary: Data indicates that the statement is true and that several of the worst affected districts are tribal ones, pointing to a higher burden of malnutrition among adivasi communities in the State
